sonnet_review.biasedText: The company has countered with support for parental approval for kids under 16 when accessing app stores and giving some apps parental controls while also being supportive of efforts in Ohio and California. Meta has also backed standards developed by the industry for age-appropriate content and standards for apps commonly used by teens.
sonnet_review.reason: The selected sentences are neutral summaries of Meta’s stated policy preferences. The article immediately follows with “But state Sen. Andrew Gounardes isn’t convinced” and a critical interview, providing counterbalance. The claim of slant rests on structural interpretation rather than biased wording in the cited text.

sonnet_review.biasedText: It is indisputable the effect social media has had on our personal well-being, on our collective well-being, how we view politics, how we view our neighbors.
sonnet_review.reason: This is a direct quote from state Sen. Andrew Gounardes in an interview segment, not the reporter’s prose. Per instructions, bias flagged from quoted sources should not be treated as the author’s bias.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Growing concerns over kids' mental health are fueling an effort in New York to combat algorithm-driven social media feeds.
sonnet_review.reason: This framing summarizes policymakers’ rationale and aims; “combat” reflects the policy goal (to restrict algorithmic feeds for minors). It does not, on its face, endorse that view or disparage alternatives. No clear elite-vs-populist bias is evident in the sentence itself.
